title: Ola vs. Sven
synopsis: Ola vs. Sven, Norway vs. Sweden — who is the best ski nation?
visual_style: European comic album style inspired by Tintin and Franco-Belgian bande dessinee. Clean, confident ink outlines with flat color fills and minimal hatching. Characters are drawn as recognizable, well-proportioned human figures — not cartoonishly exaggerated but stylized and expressive, with clear facial features and readable body language at any zoom level. The setting is a bright winter landscape: white snow, blue sky, sunshine, and crisp shadows on the ground. Backgrounds are simple and understated — snow-covered terrain, sparse pine trees, gentle hills — never competing with the two skiers for attention. The two characters dominate every panel. Camera angles vary for visual energy: close-ups of determined faces, full-body action shots of skiing and sabotage, and occasional bird's-eye views showing tiny figures on a vast white course. Color palette is dominated by white snow, bright blue sky, and the bold costume colors of the two skiers. Strictly avoid: photorealism, 3D rendering, glossy gradients, manga or anime style, overly detailed backgrounds, dark or moody lighting, and any style that pulls focus away from the characters.

locations.name: The Cross-Country Course
locations.description: A snowy cross-country skiing course on a bright, sunny winter day. The terrain varies between open snow fields, gentle hills, forest sections with tall pine trees, and narrow trail passages — but the focus is always on the two skiers, not the scenery.

characters.name: Ola Nordmann
characters.description: A sturdy, determined Norwegian cross-country skier in his late 20s with a square jaw, short brown hair, and intensely focused eyes. He wears a classic red Norwegian ski racing suit with the Norwegian flag prominently displayed on his chest. He carries ski poles and wears racing skis. His expression shifts between fierce competitive focus and smug national pride. Athletic build, strong posture, always leaning forward like he's about to charge.

characters.name: Sven Svensson
characters.description: A tall, lean Swedish cross-country skier in his late 20s with blonde hair poking out from under his ski headband, and a sharp, confident grin. He wears a blue and yellow Swedish ski racing suit with the Swedish flag prominently displayed on his chest. He carries ski poles and wears racing skis. His expression is cool and slightly smug — always acting like he's one step ahead, even when he's not. Long limbs, smooth skiing form, annoyingly graceful.

rules: The race never ends. Ola and Sven are always mid-competition, always neck-and-neck. No one ever crosses a finish line. The race simply continues forever.

Sabotage never fully works. When Ola or Sven attempts to sabotage the other — no matter how extreme (bazookas, catapults, avalanches, moose stampedes) — the victim always survives and recovers cartoonishly. The sabotage may slow them down, knock them sideways, or create chaos, but they always get back on their skis and the race continues.

Sabotage is roughly 50/50. Sometimes Ola attacks and Sven must deal with it. Other times, Sven strikes first with a cunning trick, and the user must figure out how Ola responds. The LLM should regularly have Sven initiate sabotage to keep the user on their toes.

The humor is rooted in Norway vs. Sweden rivalry — friendly, never mean-spirited. Trash talk draws on real cultural stereotypes: Norwegian brown cheese and oil money vs. Swedish meatballs and IKEA furniture. Both characters are proud of their country and love to mock the other's.

The tone is absurd, escalating, and cartoonish — think Wile E. Coyote on skis. Sabotage methods can be wildly unrealistic (rocket-powered skis, trained eagles, banana peels on snow) but the characters always react with determined sportsmanship and get back to racing.

Dialogue between Ola and Sven should be punchy trash talk, competitive banter, and nationalistic one-liners. They mock each other constantly but there's an underlying mutual respect buried deep beneath the rivalry.

Narration is delivered in the voice of an increasingly bewildered sports commentator — professional broadcasting tone struggling to maintain composure while describing someone firing a bazooka on a ski trail. Think: 'And Nordmann appears to be... unpacking what seems to be a medieval trebuchet. This is... unprecedented in Olympic history.'
long_term_narrative: The sabotage methods escalate in absurdity over time — from tripping with ski poles to increasingly ridiculous and creative schemes.
Despite the chaos, both skiers remain neck-and-neck. Every advantage is temporary. The eternal race continues.
narrative_premise: Ola Nordmann and Sven Svensson are locked in the most intense cross-country ski race in history — and it will never end. They are neck-and-neck, and both will resort to increasingly absurd sabotage to gain the upper hand. But nothing ever works for long. Bazookas misfire, avalanches are dodged, rocket skis explode at the wrong moment. The race always continues, the rivalry never dies, and the trash talk between Norway and Sweden flows as freely as the snow.
